div1.entryFree:
Ordinary, adj. 1) usual, habitual, frequent: these fits are with his highness very o. H4B IV, 4, 115. the lunacy is so o. that the whippers are in love too, As III, 2, 423.
2) common, inferior: I have no more wit than an o. man has, Tw. I, 3, 90. I saw him put down with an o. fool, I, 5, 91. my heart hath melted at a lady's tears, being an o. inundation, John V, 2, 48. H8 V, 1, 174. Caes. I, 1, 78. I, 2, 73. III, 1, 37. Lr. I, 4, 36.
